events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist Brian May was born on July 19, 1947, in Hampton, London.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the iconic Brian May! The Queen legend, astrophysicist, and all-round rock god continues to inspire. What's your favourite Queen guitar solo?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The Rolling Stones frontman Mick Jagger was born on July 26, 1943, in Dartford, Kent.

angles.name: Ageless Rocker
angles.copy: 83 years young and still performing with the energy of someone half his age! Happy birthday to the one and only Mick Jagger, the inimitable voice and swagger of The Rolling Stones.

events.title: MTV launched 45 years ago
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its first broadcast.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched and changed the music world forever. And the first video? The brilliantly ironic 'Video Killed The Radio Star' by The Buggles. Did it, though?

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe 32 years ago
events.blurb: Oasis' debut album, 'Definitely Maybe', was released on August 29, 1994, by Creation Records.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: Thirty-two years since Oasis dropped their seminal debut, 'Definitely Maybe'. An album that defined a generation and launched a Britpop phenomenon. What's your favourite track?

events.title: Woodstock Festival began 57 years ago
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music and Art Fair took place from August 15 to 18, 1969, on Max Yasgur's dairy farm in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years since half a million people gathered for '3 Days of Peace & Music' at Woodstock. An iconic moment in music history that shaped a generation.

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', passed away on August 16, 1977.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: On this day, we remember the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley, who left us on August 16, 1977. His music and legend live on.

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's death
events.blurb: Influential rapper Tupac Shakur died on September 13, 1996, in Las Vegas, after being shot six days earlier.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering Tupac Shakur, who tragically passed away on this day in 1996. His powerful lyrics and enduring legacy continue to resonate.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death
events.blurb: Legendary guitarist Jimi Hendrix died on September 18, 1970, in London, at the age of 27.

angles.name: Guitar Genius
angles.copy: On this day, we remember the unparalleled genius of Jimi Hendrix, who left us too soon in 1970. His revolutionary guitar playing changed music forever.

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road 57 years ago
events.blurb: The Beatles' iconic album 'Abbey Road' was released on September 26, 1969, in the UK.

angles.name: Iconic Album Cover
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years since The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover is almost as famous as the music within it. What's your favourite track from the record?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The Pretenders frontwoman Chrissie Hynde was born on September 7, 1951, in Akron, Ohio.

angles.name: Rock Icon
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the formidable Chrissie Hynde! The voice and attitude of The Pretenders, still rocking after all these years.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Oasis and Beady Eye frontman Liam Gallagher was born on September 21, 1972, in Manchester.

angles.name: Rock 'n' Roll Star
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Still bringing that unmistakable swagger and voice to the world. As you were.

events.title: John Lennon released 'Imagine' 55 years ago
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic single 'Imagine' was released on October 11, 1971.

angles.name: Imagine Peace
angles.copy: Fifty-five years since John Lennon gave us 'Imagine', a timeless anthem for peace and unity. Its message resonates as strongly today as it ever did.

events.title: Apple released the first iPod 25 years ago
events.blurb: The first Apple iPod was unveiled on October 23, 2001, promising '1,000 songs in your pocket'.

angles.name: Music in Your Pocket
angles.copy: Twenty-five years ago, Apple introduced the first iPod and changed how we listen to music forever. Who remembers their first iPod?

events.title: It's James Bond Day!
events.blurb: October 5th marks James Bond Day, celebrating the release of the first 007 film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Celebrating 007, the iconic spy who's given us decades of thrilling cinema and some truly unforgettable theme tunes. What's your favourite Bond film or song?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: The legendary Beatles songwriter and peace activist John Lennon was born on October 9, 1940, in Liverpool.

angles.name: Lennon's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering John Lennon, who would have been 86 today. His vision for a better world and his timeless music continue to inspire generations.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: British comedy legend John Cleese was born on October 27, 1939, in Weston-super-Mare, Somerset.

angles.name: Comedy Genius
angles.copy: Happy 87th birthday to the brilliant John Cleese! From Monty Python to Fawlty Towers, his unique brand of humour has brought joy to millions.
